subject: Rental License Inspection — 1402 Granville Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I received a notice that my rental property at 1402 Granville Ave is due for its annual rental license inspection. I've owned this property for nine years and have always been up to code. I'd like to schedule at a time that works around my tenant's schedule — she works nights and sleeps until noon. Can your office point me to the right contact to coordinate the timing?

subject: Rental License Inspection — 1402 Granville Ave
body_clean: Mr. Shelton, under Village Code §11-3-7 annual interior inspections are required before license renewal. I can accommodate an afternoon slot — typically 45 minutes. I have openings Thursday, April 17th at 1:00 p.m. or Friday, April 18th at 2:30 p.m. Please reply with your preference and I'll confirm. Your tenant does not need to be present, but access to all interior spaces including the basement and utility closet is required.

subject: Re-inspection Request After Violations at 3318 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Good afternoon. I received violation notice #2024-CE-0418 on October 2nd citing peeling exterior paint and a broken handrail at my rental on Harvard Ave. Both have been corrected — the painter finished last Friday and the railing was replaced the week before. My compliance window closes October 28th and I'd like to get the re-inspection booked right away. Can I schedule it online or do I need to call?

subject: Re-inspection Request After Violations at 3318 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Ms. Osei, I have your case on file under §15-2-11. Re-inspections aren't on the online portal — please use phone or email. I can fit you in Wednesday, October 23rd at 10:00 a.m., giving a five-day buffer before your deadline. Please reply to confirm and have contractor receipts or photos available at inspection time.

subject: Occupancy Permit Needed Before Closing — 825 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm purchasing a two-flat at 825 Bohland Ave and my attorney just told me the Village requires a municipal inspection and occupancy certificate before we can close. Closing is January 30th. My realtor didn't flag this until now. Is there any way to get an inspection in the next two weeks? I don't want to push the closing date but I understand this may be a stretch.

subject: Occupancy Permit Needed Before Closing — 825 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Mr. Hutchins, point-of-sale inspections for residential transfers are required under §10-5-2. The fee is $85 for a two-unit structure, payable at Village Hall before I arrive. I can schedule Monday, January 20th at 9:00 a.m., leaving time to address minor findings before your closing. If the property passes — or if conditions are noted and accepted in writing by the buyer — we issue the certificate same day. Bring a completed inspection request form and your payment.

subject: Repeat Code Violations — Rental Property at 710 Morris Ave
body_clean: I'm writing about a rental property at 710 Morris Ave. This is the second violation notice posted on that door in six months — first the overgrown yard, now a deteriorating soffit with nesting birds. I live next door and stare at exposed wood every day. I understand there's a process, but at what point does repeated non-compliance result in real consequences? I'd appreciate a direct answer.

subject: Repeat Code Violations — Rental Property at 710 Morris Ave
body_clean: Ms. Balasko, thank you for writing and I understand your frustration. I've asked Code Enforcement to pull the complete history on 710 Morris Ave. A second violation within 12 months triggers a mandatory compliance hearing before the Village's Property Standards Board, with fines up to $500 per day if the owner fails to appear or remedy. Officer Pulaski will be in touch with you directly. You have every right to expect your street to be properly maintained.

subject: Framing Inspection Request — 2204 Washington Blvd, Permit BP-2025-0892
body_clean: Good morning. Verdi Excavating and Drainage is the general on a rear addition at 2204 Washington Blvd under permit #BP-2025-0892. We're ready for framing inspection and need to schedule before the window closes September 26th. Our site super is available any weekday morning next week. Footing and rough-grade inspections passed without comment. Please advise on availability.

subject: No Heat in Rental Unit — 519 50th Ave Unit 2
body_clean: I rent Unit 2 at 519 50th Ave. My heat has been out since February 4th. I've texted my landlord six times and he keeps saying the part is on order — it's been three weeks now. I have a five-year-old in this apartment. Does the Village have any authority over heating in rentals? I don't want to lose my lease but I can't keep running space heaters. A code number I found online went to voicemail. Please help.

subject: No Heat in Rental Unit — 519 50th Ave Unit 2
body_clean: Ms. Ferreira, the Village absolutely has authority here. Under §11-4-9, rental units must maintain 68°F interior heat between October 1st and May 1st. I am scheduling an emergency habitability inspection for your unit tomorrow morning. If it fails the heat standard, your landlord receives a same-day violation notice with a 48-hour correction window before daily fines begin. Please be available to let me in between 9:00 and 10:00 a.m. Illinois law protects you from retaliation for filing this complaint.

subject: Permit for Detached Garage — 1617 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Hi, I'd like to build a detached two-car garage at 1617 Hirsch Ave. A neighbor said I need a permit and an inspection before pouring the slab. I've never pulled a permit in Bellwood. Do I need architect-stamped drawings for something this straightforward, and roughly how long does approval take? I'd like to start before end of summer if there's any way to make that happen.

subject: No Update on Occupancy Permit — 2041 Frederick Ave
body_clean: I submitted an occupancy permit application for 2041 Frederick Ave on October 18th and was told to expect a decision in 10 business days. It's now been four weeks and I've heard nothing. I called twice and left voicemails with no callback. My moving date is November 22nd. Could someone please check on this? I just need to know whether to proceed or push my plans.

subject: No Update on Occupancy Permit — 2041 Frederick Ave
body_clean: Ms. Mensah, I apologize — the missed callbacks are not acceptable and I'm sorry for the anxiety this has caused. I pulled your file this morning. The inspection was completed October 30th and passed, but the certificate was waiting on the Building Official's signature while he was out on leave. I'm walking it to his desk right now. You should have your occupancy certificate by end of day today or first thing tomorrow. You are clear to proceed with your November 22nd move.

subject: Thank You — Rental Inspection at 617 25th Ave
body_clean: Just a short note to pass along some appreciation. The inspector who came to 617 25th Ave last Tuesday was thorough but fair — he explained everything without being condescending, and I didn't feel like I was being put through the wringer. I had one water-heater issue and he walked me right through what met code and what didn't. I made the correction by Friday and the license renewed without drama. That kind of professionalism makes a real difference. Please thank him on my behalf.

subject: Inspection Scheduling Process — Active Permits on Bellwood and Eastern
body_clean: Good morning. I'm a licensed general contractor with two active Bellwood permits — a bathroom addition on Bellwood Ave and a basement finish on Eastern Ave. I want to understand your inspection scheduling protocol: online requests, 24-hour notice, same-day calls? Also, does one inspector cover all residential trades or are electrical and plumbing handled separately? I'm trying to build this into my crew scheduling workflow to avoid wasted site trips.
